justification: All 5 ACs implemented with correct HTTP codes and error codes (400/INVALID_EMAIL_FORMAT, 404/USER_NOT_FOUND, 409/EMAIL_ALREADY_IN_USE, 200 for valid update and idempotent same-email). AC5 idempotency is distinctly handled from AC2 conflict check — same-email short-circuits before findByEmail is ever called. No external validation library; uses simple regex. Tests cover all 5 ACs with explicit AC-numbered test names. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific tests. files_changed lists handler and test files.

observations.text: AC5 idempotency correctly handled as a separate guard before the AC2 conflict check, avoiding both false 409s and unnecessary DB calls
Guard ordering is deliberate and tested — format validation before DB lookups, idempotency before conflict check
Email validation uses simple regex per spec, no external library introduced
Error responses include both HTTP status codes and structured error code fields
AC3 tested with 7 parameterized edge cases beyond the minimum requirement
Extra guard ordering test verifies AC3 takes priority over AC4 when both apply

## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** `PATCH /users/{id}/email` Express.js route handler with injected `UserRepository`, written in TypeScript with a parallel JS version.

**Approach:** Factory function pattern (`makePatchUserEmail(repo)`) — the repository is injected at construction time and the returned async function is the Express handler. Guard clauses execute in a deliberate order: format validation → existence check → id
